examples.title: One-Sample T-Test Implementation
examples.description: Calculate t-statistic and p-value for one-sample t-test
examples.code:
import math
from scipy import stats

def one_sample_t_test(sample_data, pop_mean):
    n = len(sample_data)
    sample_mean = sum(sample_data) / n
    
    # Calculate sample standard deviation
    variance = sum((x - sample_mean)**2 for x in sample_data) / (n - 1)
    sample_std = math.sqrt(variance)
    
    # Calculate standard error
    std_error = sample_std / math.sqrt(n)
    
    # Calculate t-statistic
    t_statistic = (sample_mean - pop_mean) / std_error
    
    # Degrees of freedom
    df = n - 1
    
    # Calculate p-value (two-tailed)
    p_value = 2 * (1 - stats.t.cdf(abs(t_statistic), df))
    
    return {
        't_statistic': t_statistic,
        'p_value': p_value,
        'degrees_of_freedom': df,
        'sample_mean': sample_mean,
        'std_error': std_error
    }

# Example usage
sample = [78, 82, 75, 80, 77, 79, 81, 76, 83, 74]
pop_mean = 72
result = one_sample_t_test(sample, pop_mean)
print(f"t-statistic: {result['t_statistic']:.4f}")
print(f"p-value: {result['p_value']:.4f}")
examples.explanation: This Python code implements a one-sample t-test by calculating the t-statistic using the formula t = (x̄ - μ) / (s/√n), then computing the p-value using the t-cumulative distribution function.

examples.title: Two-Sample Independent T-Test Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for performing independent samples t-test with equal variances
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TwoSampleTTest
INPUT: sample1[], sample2[], alpha
BEGIN
  n1 = LENGTH(sample1)
  n2 = LENGTH(sample2)
  
  mean1 = CALCULATE_MEAN(sample1)
  mean2 = CALCULATE_MEAN(sample2)
  
  var1 = CALCULATE_VARIANCE(sample1, mean1)
  var2 = CALCULATE_VARIANCE(sample2, mean2)
  
  df = n1 + n2 - 2
  
  # Pooled variance
  pooled_var = ((n1-1)*var1 + (n2-1)*var2) / df
  
  # Standard error
  se = SQRT(pooled_var * (1/n1 + 1/n2))
  
  # t-statistic
  t_stat = (mean1 - mean2) / se
  
  # Critical t-value
  t_critical = LOOKUP_T_TABLE(alpha/2, df)
  
  # Decision
  IF ABS(t_stat) > t_critical THEN
    PRINT "Reject null hypothesis"
  ELSE
    PRINT "Fail to reject null hypothesis"
  ENDIF
  
  RETURN t_stat, p_value
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the two-sample independent t-test procedure. It calculates pooled variance, standard error, t-statistic, and compares with critical value to make a decision about the null hypothesis.

examples.title: T-Distribution Critical Value Calculator
examples.description: Calculate critical t-value for given significance level and degrees of freedom using approximation
examples.explanation: This C program calculates the t-statistic for a one-sample t-test and uses an approximation formula to find critical t-values. It demonstrates the core calculation: t = (x̄ - μ) / (s/√n).

examples.title: Confidence Interval Using T-Distribution
examples.description: Calculate confidence interval for population mean using t-distribution
examples.code:
import math
from scipy import stats

def confidence_interval_t(sample_data, confidence_level=0.95):
    n = len(sample_data)
    mean = sum(sample_data) / n
    
    # Sample standard deviation
    variance = sum((x - mean)**2 for x in sample_data) / (n - 1)
    std_dev = math.sqrt(variance)
    
    # Standard error
    std_error = std_dev / math.sqrt(n)
    
    # Degrees of freedom
    df = n - 1
    
    # Critical t-value
    alpha = 1 - confidence_level
    t_critical = stats.t.ppf(1 - alpha/2, df)
    
    # Margin of error
    margin_of_error = t_critical * std_error
    
    # Confidence interval
    lower_bound = mean - margin_of_error
    upper_bound = mean + margin_of_error
    
    return {
        'mean': mean,
        'confidence_level': confidence_level,
        'margin_of_error': margin_of_error,
        'lower_bound': lower_bound,
        'upper_bound': upper_bound,
        't_critical': t_critical,
        'degrees_of_freedom': df
    }

# Example: Test scores from 15 students
scores = [78, 82, 75, 80, 77, 79, 81, 76, 83, 74, 79, 85, 72, 80, 77]
result = confidence_interval_t(scores, 0.95)

print(f"Sample Mean: {result['mean']:.2f}")
print(f"95% Confidence Interval: ({result['lower_bound']:.2f}, {result['upper_bound']:.2f})")
print(f"Margin of Error: ±{result['margin_of_error']:.2f}")
print(f"Degrees of Freedom: {result['degrees_of_freedom']}")
print(f"t-critical Value: {result['t_critical']:.4f}")
examples.explanation: This Python code calculates the confidence interval for population mean using t-distribution. The formula is CI = x̄ ± t_α/2 × (s/√n), where t_α/2 is found using the t-distribution's inverse CDF.
